member.name: F:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ExportSettings.prefabSavePaths
member.summary:
The path where Convert To Model will save the new fbx and prefab.
To help teams work together, this is stored to be relative to the Application.dataPath, and the path separator is the forward-slash (e.g. unix and http, not windows).
Use GetRelativeSavePath / SetRelativeSavePath to get/set this value, properly interpreted for the current platform.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ExportSettings.ConvertToAssetRelativePath(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Convert an absolute path into a relative path like what you would get from GetRelativeSavePath.
This uses '/' as the path separator.
If 'requireSubdirectory' is the default on, return empty-string if the full path is not in a subdirectory of assets.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ExportSettings.GetRelativePath(System.String,System.String,System.Char)
member.summary: Compute how to get from 'fromDir' to 'toDir' via a relative path.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ExportSettings.NormalizePath(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Char)
member.summary:
Normalize a path, cleaning up path separators, resolving '.' and '..', removing duplicate and trailing path separators, etc.
If the path passed in is a relative path, we remove leading path separators. If it's an absolute path we don't.
If you claim the path is absolute but actually it's relative, we treat it as a relative path.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.GetMeshForComponent`1
member.summary:
If your MonoBehaviour knows about some custom geometry that isn't in a MeshFilter or SkinnedMeshRenderer, use RegisterMeshCallback to get a callback when the exporter tries to export your component.
The callback should return true, and output the mesh you want.
Return false if you don't want to drive this game object.
Return true and output a null mesh if you don't want the exporter to output anything.

member.name: T:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.GetMeshForObject
member.summary:
Delegate used to convert a GameObject into a mesh.
This is useful if you want to have broader control over the export process than the GetMeshForComponent callbacks provide. But it's less efficient because you'll get a callback on every single GameObject.

summary.para: Use the ModelExporter class to export Unity GameObjects to an FBX file.
Use the ExportObject and ExportObjects methods. The default export options are used when exporting the objects to the FBX file.
For API overview and example scripts, refer to @api_index.

member.summary: Export the mesh's blend shapes.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ModelExporter.ConvertToFbxVector4(UnityEngine.Vector3,System.Single)
member.summary:
Takes in a left-handed UnityEngine.Vector3 denoting a normal, returns a right-handed FbxVector4.
Unity is left-handed, Maya and Max are right-handed. The FbxSdk conversion routines can't handle changing handedness.
Remember you also need to flip the winding order on your polygons.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ModelExporter.ExportTexture(UnityEngine.Material,System.String,Autodesk.Fbx.FbxSurfaceMaterial,System.String)
member.summary:
Exports a texture from Unity to FBX. The texture must be a property on the unityMaterial; it gets linked to the FBX via a property on the fbxMaterial.
The texture file must be a file on disk; it is not embedded within the FBX.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ModelExporter.GetMaterialColor(UnityEngine.Material,System.String,System.Single)
member.summary: Get the color of a material, or grey if we can't find it.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ModelExporter.ExportMaterial(UnityEngine.Material,Autodesk.Fbx.FbxScene,Autodesk.Fbx.FbxNode)
member.summary: Export (and map) a Unity PBS material to FBX classic material

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ModelExporter.AssignLayerElementMaterial(Autodesk.Fbx.FbxMesh,UnityEngine.Mesh,System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets up the material to polygon mapping for fbxMesh. To determine which part of the mesh uses which material, look at the submeshes and which polygons they represent. Assuming equal number of materials as submeshes, and that they are in the same order. (i.e. submesh 1 uses material 1)

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ModelExporter.ExportMesh(UnityEngine.Mesh,Autodesk.Fbx.FbxNode,UnityEngine.Material[])
member.summary:
Exports a unity mesh and attaches it to the node as an FbxMesh.

Gets the count of animated objects to be exported.
In addition, collects the minimum set of what needs to be exported for each GameObject hierarchy. This contains all the animated GameObjects, their ancestors, their transforms, as well as any animated components and the animation clips. Also, the first animation to export, if any.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ModelExporter.MeshForComponentCallbacks
member.summary: Map from type (must be a MonoBehaviour) to callback. The type safety is lost; the caller must ensure it at run-time.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ModelExporter.RegisterMeshCallback``1(UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.GetMeshForComponent{``0},System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Register a callback to invoke if the object has a component of type T.
This function is prefered over the other mesh callback registration methods because it's type-safe, efficient, and invocation order between types can be controlled in the UI by reordering the components.
It's an error to register a callback for a component that already has one, unless 'replace' is set to true.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ModelExporter.RegisterMeshCallback(System.Type,UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.GetMeshForComponent,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Register a callback to invoke if the object has a component of type T.
The callback will be invoked with an argument of type T, it's safe to downcast.
Normally you'll want to use the generic form, but this one is easier to use with reflection.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ModelExporter.UnRegisterMeshCallback``1
member.summary: Forget the callback linked to a component of type T.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ModelExporter.UnRegisterMeshCallback(System.Type)
member.summary: Forget the callback linked to a component of type T.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ModelExporter.UnRegisterAllMeshCallbacks
member.summary: Forget the callbacks linked to components.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ModelExporter.RegisterMeshObjectCallback(UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.GetMeshForObject)
member.summary:
Register a callback to invoke on every GameObject we export.
Avoid doing this if you can use a callback that depends on type.
The GameObject-based callbacks are checked before the component-based ones.
Multiple GameObject-based callbacks can be registered; they are checked in order of registration.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ModelExporter.UnRegisterMeshObjectCallback(UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.GetMeshForObject)
member.summary: Forget a GameObject-based callback.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ModelExporter.UnRegisterAllMeshObjectCallbacks
member.summary: Forget all GameObject-based callbacks.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.FbxPropertyChannelPair.GetUnityPropertyChannelPair(System.String)
member.summary:
Separates and returns the property and channel from the full Unity property name.
Takes what is after the last period as the channel. In order to use this have to be certain that there are channels, as there are cases where what is after the last period is still the property name. E.g. m_Sources.Array.data[0].weight has no channel.

member.summary:
Get the Fbx property name for the given Unity constraint source property name from the given list.
This is different from GetFbxProperty() because the Unity constraint source properties contain indices, and the Fbx constraint source property contains the name of the source object.
member.returns: The Fbx property name or null if there was no match in the list

summary.para: Class for converting an exported FBX to a Prefab Variant.
For API overview and example scripts, refer to @api_index.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.OnGameObjectContextItem(UnityEditor.MenuCommand)
member.summary:
OnContextItem is called either: * when the user selects the menu item via the top menu (with a null MenuCommand), or * when the user selects the menu item via the context menu (in which case there's a context)
OnContextItem gets called once per selected object (if the parent and child are selected, then OnContextItem will only be called on the parent)

member.summary: Validate the menu items defined above.

member.summary: Gets the export settings.

member.summary: Return true if the given set contains only prefab assets on disk, and nothing from the scene.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.CreateInstantiatedModelPrefab(UnityEngine.GameObject[])
member.summary:
Create instantiated model prefabs from a selection of objects.
Every hierarchy in the selection will be exported, under the name of the root.
If an object and one of its descendents are both selected, the descendent is not promoted to be a prefab -- we only export the root.
member.param: Unity game objects to convert to Model Prefab instances
Path to save Model Prefab; use FbxExportSettings if null

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.FixSceneReferenceToObject(UnityEngine.Object,UnityEngine.GameObject,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{UnityEngine.Object,UnityEngine.Object})
member.summary:
For sceneObj, replace references of objects that are keys in fixSceneRefsMap, to the value.
If the scene object is toConvertRoot or a child of it, then do not fix its references as it will be deleted after conversion.
member.param: scene object with reference to replace
mapping from original object to new object
root of the hierarchy being converted

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.GetPropertyReflection(System.Object,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Helper for getting a property from an instance with reflection.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.ConvertToPrefabVariant(UnityEngine.GameObject,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToPrefabVariantOptions)
member.summary:
Convert one object (and the hierarchy below it) to a prefab variant of a model prefab.
Returns the prefab asset that's linked to the fbx.
If 'toConvert' is:
A GameObject in the Scene, then the method exports the hierarchy to an FBX and creates a new Prefab Variant pointing to the exported FBX.
The root of an FBX asset, or the root of an instance of an FBX asset, then the method creates a new Prefab Variant pointing to the existing FBX.
A Prefab asset, then the method exports a new FBX asset and creates a new Prefab Variant pointing to the FBX.
member.returns: The prefab variant linked to an fbx file.
member.param: Absolute platform-specific path to the fbx file. If the file already exists, it will be overwritten. May be null, in which case we construct a unique filename. Ignored if 'toConvert' is an fbx asset or is an instance of one.

Absolute platform-specific path to a directory in which to put the fbx file under a unique filename. May be null, in which case we use the export settings. Ignored if 'fbxFullPath' is specified. Ignored if 'toConvert' is an fbx asset or an instance of one.

Absolute platform-specific path to the prefab file. If the file already exists, it will be overwritten. May be null, in which case we construct a unique filename. Ignored if 'toConvert' is a prefab asset.

Absolute platform-specific path to a directory in which to put the prefab file under a unique filename. May be null, in which case we use the export settings. Ignored if 'prefabFullPath' is specified. Ignored if 'toConvert' is a prefab asset.
Export options to use for exporting the model asset to convert to a Prefab.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.SourceObjectInfo.destGO
member.summary: The exported GameObject that will replace the source.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.SourceObjectInfo.sceneObjectsWithReference
member.summary:
A list of scene objects that reference this object or one of its components. Populated before traversing the components to speed up the search time of finding/replacing references.
Note: In older versions of Unity (without the Search API), searching for references by GameObject ID will not return objects that reference the components of the same GameObject. Therefore in older versions still need to do a search by component.

member.name: F:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.s_nameToInfo
member.summary: Dictionary from name of source object (in toConvert hierarchy) to info.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.GatherSceneHierarchy(UnityEngine.GameObject)
member.summary: Populates the s_nameToInfo dictionary by traversing the given hierarchy. In newer versions of Unity, as it traverses, will also perform a search for references to each object or component in the scene.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.WillExportFbx(UnityEngine.GameObject)
member.summary: Check whether Convert will be exporting an fbx file, or reusing one.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.GetOrCreateFbxAsset(UnityEngine.GameObject,System.String,System.String,UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToPrefabSettingsSerialize)
member.summary:
Return an FBX asset that corresponds to 'toConvert'.
If 'toConvert' is the root of an FBX asset, return it.
If it's an instance in a scene the points to the root of an FBX asset, return that asset.
Otherwise, export according to the paths and options, and return the new asset.
member.param: GameObject for which we want an fbx asset
Export will choose an appropriate filename in this directory. Ignored if fbxFullPath is set. Ignored if toConvert is an fbx asset or an instance of an fbx.
Export will create this file. Overrides fbxDirectoryFullPath. Ignored if toConvert is an fbx asset or an instance of an fbx.
member.returns: The root of a model prefab asset.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.GetFbxAssetOrNull(UnityEngine.GameObject)
member.summary:
Returns the fbx asset on disk corresponding to the same hierarchy as is selected.
Returns go if go is the root of a model prefab. Returns the prefab parent of go if it's the root of a model prefab. Returns null in all other circumstances.
member.returns: The root of a model prefab asset, or null.
member.param: A gameobject either in the scene or in the assets folder.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.IncrementFileName(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Check if the file exists, and if it does, then increment the name. e.g. if filename is Sphere.fbx and it already exists, change it to Sphere 1.fbx.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.EnforceUniqueNames(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{UnityEngine.GameObject})
member.summary: Enforces that all object names be unique before exporting. If an object with a duplicate name is found, then it is incremented. e.g. Sphere becomes Sphere 1

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.UpdateFromSourceRecursive(UnityEngine.GameObject,UnityEngine.GameObject)
member.summary: Updates the meshes and materials of the exported GameObjects to link to those imported from the FBX.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.MapNameToSourceRecursive(UnityEngine.GameObject,UnityEngine.GameObject)
member.summary:
Gets a dictionary linking dest GameObject name to source game object.
Before export we ensure that the hierarchy has unique names, so that we can map by name afterwards.
member.returns: Dictionary containing the name to source game object.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.CopySerializedProperty(UnityEditor.SerializedObject,UnityEditor.SerializedProperty)
member.summary: Copy the object reference from fromProperty to the matching property on serializedObject. Use nameMap to find the correct object reference to use.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.Formats.Fbx.Exporter.ConvertToNestedPrefab.CopyComponents(UnityEngine.GameObject,UnityEngine.GameObject,UnityEngine.GameObject,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{UnityEngine.Object,UnityEngine.Object})
member.summary:
Copy components on the 'from' object which is the object being converted, over to the 'to' object which is the FBX.
Copy over everything except meshes and materials, since these are already in the FBX.
The 'from' hierarchy is not modified.
Note: 'root' is the root object that is being converted
For each component, add to fixSceneRefsMap a mapping from the original component on "from" to the new component on "to". In order to be able to fix scene references that were pointing to the original component.
